title: Senior Video Editor — Top 1% Only (Full Time | Remote | Philippines | EST Hours)
description:
Senior Video Editor — Top 1% Only (Full Time | Remote | Philippines | EST Hours)

We are a high-performance U.S.-based marketing and media group working with fast-growing home service brands (roofing, HVAC, plumbing, electrical) and competitive sports brands (athletes, academies, teams, sports tech companies).

We are not looking for average editors.

We are looking for a hungry, fast, creative, agency-tested Senior Video Editor who takes ownership, moves quickly, and understands that video is a performance tool — not just an art project.

If you need constant direction, slow timelines, or safe workloads… this role is not for you.

If you want to grow, be challenged, and work with serious marketers — keep reading.

________________________________________

What You Will Actually Be Doing

• Editing high-volume short-form content (Reels / TikTok / Shorts / paid ads)

• Producing performance marketing creatives that drive leads and engagement

• Editing YouTube content and brand storytelling videos

• Adding motion graphics, captions, pacing, sound design, and visual hooks

• Optimizing videos for watch time, retention, and conversion

• Handling multiple clients and fast turnaround expectations

• Executing creative direction — and improving it with your own ideas

• Owning projects from raw footage to final export

This is an agency environment. Deadlines matter. Speed matters. Results matter.

________________________________________

Minimum Requirements (Non-Negotiable)

• 5+ years professional video editing experience

• Proven experience working inside a marketing or creative agency

• Strong portfolio that shows:

o Short-form social edits

o Paid ad creatives

o Long-form YouTube or brand videos

• Ability to work full U.S. Eastern Standard Time hours

• Ability to work without micromanagement

• Ability to receive direct feedback and implement revisions quickly

• Strong English communication skills (verbal and written)

• High personal accountability and reliability

________________________________________

Required Software (You Must Already Own & Be Advanced In At Least One)

• Adobe Premiere Pro

• Adobe After Effects

• DaVinci Resolve

• Final Cut Pro

Strong preference if you also use:

• CapCut (advanced social editing workflows)

• Motion graphics plugins

• Sound design libraries

• LUT / color grading workflows

• 
 / Google Drive collaboration systems

You must have:

• A powerful editing workstation

• High-speed stable internet

• Ability to handle large file transfers daily

________________________________________

What Separates Top 1?itors From Everyone Else

• You understand marketing psychology and pacing

• You can create scroll-stopping hooks in the first 1–3 seconds

• You edit for performance — not just aesthetics

• You move fast without sacrificing quality

• You anticipate what the strategist or client wants before being told

• You take pride in ownership and delivery

• You want upward mobility — not just tasks

________________________________________

Growth Path (Real Opportunity)

Top performers can grow into:

• Lead Video Editor

• Motion Graphics Specialist

• Creative Strategist Support

• Video Team Manager

• Content Performance Director

We promote based on output, ownership, and results.

________________________________________

Application Instructions (READ CAREFULLY — This Filters Serious Candidates)

To be considered, you must submit:

1. Resume

2. Portfolio link showing:

o Short-form social content

o Paid ad creatives

o Long-form edits

3. A short video response answering ALL of the following:

o Your agency experience (clients / industries)

o Your strongest editing software

o Your experience working EST hours

o Your average turnaround time on a 60-second social video

o The word “Performance” somewhere in your application subject line

Applications missing any of the above will not be reviewed.

________________________________________

This is a long-term full-time role for someone who wants to grow with a serious U.S. marketing organization.

If you are fast, reliable, creative, and accountable — we want to work with you.

title: Product Manager
description:
Company: BriarBear

Location:  Fully Remote (Philippines)

Schedule: Graveyard Shift (Start time between 6:00 PM - 8:00 PM PH Time)

Employment Type:   Full-time



About Yibby



Yibby is an AI-powered gift-recommendation chatbot that helps people find the perfect gift for

anyone in seconds. Users tell Yibby who they’re shopping for — for example, “my dad, born in

1980, loves anime, birthday gift” — and Yibby suggests thoughtful, personalized gifts based on

interests, demographics, personality, seasonality, and more.



Yibby’s recommendation engine is powered by a curated gift database and supported through

affiliate partnerships with Amazon, Target, Etsy, TikTok Shop, and others. Our mission is to

make gift-giving joyful, personal, and effortless.



About the Role



We’re hiring a Product Manager who will also serve as Yibby’s “Gift Master” — the human

curator responsible for making sure Yibby recommends amazing, high-quality gifts.

This is a hands-on role focused on building and maintaining the universe of gifts that Yibby can

recommend. You will ensure that every item in our system is:



- A genuinely great gift

- High-quality

- In-stock and up to date

- Seasonally appropriate

- Eligible for strong affiliate commissions

- Matched to the correct recipient profiles and occasions



Your Responsibilities Include



1.  Curating the Gift Catalog



- Evaluating products across major affiliate marketplaces

- Determining which items make excellent gifts (vs. generic products)

- Tagging and categorizing gifts by age, interests, hobbies, personality, and special

occasions

- Prioritizing high-commission, high-delight items



2.  Optimizing Based on Business Goals



- Filtering out low-commission, low-quality, or unavailable items

- Tracking seasonal trends and upcoming holidays

- Identifying trending items, viral products, and new releases





3. Maintaining the Gift Database



- Creating structured attributes and metadata for each product

- Improving the taxonomy of categories, tags, and matching logic

- Ensuring the recommendation engine has the right inputs to select great gifts





4.  Reviewing Gift Content



You will collaborate with Yibby’s content team responsible for producing:

- Product photos

- Lifestyle images

- Short videos

- AI-generated UGC or demos

Your role is to ensure the content is accurate, appealing, and suited to the gifting

scenario.





5.  Working with Product & Engineering



- Presenting new gift categories or seasonal collections

- Partnering with engineering to refine the recommendation engine

- Translating product ideas into structured requirements or data needs

Yibby currently does not have a dedicated UX designer, so you will sometimes use AI

tools or simple mockups to visualize flows, data structures, or tagging systems.



What We’re Looking For

- Experience in product management, e-commerce, merchandising, or similar

- Strong research skills and attention to detail

- An instinct for what makes a great gift

- Excellent written English and communication skills

- Comfort using AI tools for research or content creation

- Highly organized with the ability to work independently



Nice to Have: 

- Experience with affiliate programs (Amazon Associates, TikTok Shop, etc.)

- Background in e-commerce catalog management

- Knowledge of gifting behavior, consumer trends, or product psychology

- Experience reviewing or creating product photos or short-form content

- Interest in recommendation systems or personalization engines 



Apply thru this form -

title: Marketing Researcher
description:
Company: BriarBear

Location: Fully Remote (Philippines)

Schedule: Graveyard Shift (Start time between 5:00 PM - 7:00 PM PH Time)

Employment Type: Full-Time



About this Role



We are hiring a Marketing Researcher to own and drive long-burn, compounding organic

growth for a portfolio of AI-powered digital products.



BriarBear builds consumer products, SaaS tools, and mobile apps with AI at their core—AI

chatbots with proprietary knowledge bases, intelligent workflows, and AI-powered matching

systems. These products live or die by distribution.



While some roles on our team focus on short-form content and social platforms, this role is

responsible for the slower, deeper, compounding channels:



- SEO & organic search

- Blogs (strategy, not writing)

-
 campaigns

- Pinterest

- App Store Optimization (ASO)

- Authority, backlinks, and PR-driven discoverability

This is not a writing role.

This is a strategy, systems, and accountability role for someone who thinks like a growth

engineer—someone who can turn traffic data, rankings, and funnels into clear plans and

measurable outcomes.

You are responsible for d
 what we should rank for, why it matters, how we get there,

and how we measure success.



What this Role is Accountable For



This role is accountable for real business outcomes, not output:

- Traffic growth

- Search rankings

- Conversion rates

- Authority and visibility

- Revenue impact

Views without conversion are failure, not success.



What You’ll Be Responsible For



1. Own Long-Burn Organic Growth Strategy



Develop and continuously refine organic growth strategies across:

- Google Search (SEO)

- Blogs (topic selection, optimization, performance)

-
 campaigns

- Pinterest

- App Store rankings (ASO)

- Emerging discovery surfaces (AI search / LLMs)

You are expected to understand why something ranks, not just that it ranks.



2. Translate Business Goals Into Measurable Growth Targets



You will take high-level goals such as:

- “Increase organic traffic from 20K ? 100K monthly users”

- “Rank for high-intent keywords in X category”

- “Increase app store installs from organic search”

And translate them into:

- Keyword targets and ranking goals

- Traffic and conversion benchmarks

- Funnel assumptions (impressions ? clicks ? users ? customers)

- Clear milestones and timelines

You are accountable for defining the strategy and path forward.



3. SEO, Keywords, and Authority Strategy



You will:

- Analyze current traffic sources and performance

- Identify keyword opportunities worth pursuing (and which are not)

- Build keyword maps and content opportunity lists

- D
 what content should exist to capture search demand

- Optimize existing content to improve rankings and conversion

This includes authority-building strategy:

- Backlink acquisition (organic, partnerships, PR, tools like Outrank, etc.)

- D
 whether to pursue media placements, PR, or direct backlink strategies

- Building topical authority in our category



You will help answer questions like:



- What keywords are we ranking for today?

- What keywords should we rank for next?

- How do we become a trusted authority in this space?



4. Funnel Thinking & Conversion Optimization

Design and test strategies that convert:

- Search impressions ? website visits

- Website visits ? signups

- Signups ? paying customers

D
 and test:

- Content intent (informational vs. transactional)

- Page structure and optimization

- Website vs. app store routing

-
 sequencing and timing 



Monitor conversion rates at each stage and adjust strategy accordingly.



5. Measurement, Reporting, and Iteration



Track and report on:

- Traffic by channel

- Keyword rankings

- Conversion rates

- Performance by content category



You will:



- Build dashboards and reports leadership can understand

- Identify what’s working and double down

- Kill underperforming strategies quickly

- Diagnose why growth stalls and propose fixes

You own the outcome.



6. Work With (But Do Not Replace) Creative & Writing Resources



You will not be expected to write blogs or long-form content yourself.

Instead, you will:

- Provide clear briefs to writers or creators

- Define keyword intent, structure, and success criteria

- Review performance and iterate based on data



Who You Are



- Strategic thinker with a strong bias toward execution

- Analytical and systems-oriented

- Comfortable owning numbers and being accountable for results



- Understands SEO, authority, and ranking mechanics deeply or willing to learn

- Can explain why a strategy should work

- Organized, detail-oriented, and process-driven

- Comfortable working directly with leadership



Nice to Have (But Not Required)



- Proven experience in SEO, growth marketing, or performance marketing

- Strong understanding of:

- Search intent

- Keywords and rankings

- Funnels and conversion metrics

- Experience analyzing traffic and performance data

- Familiarity with ASO (App Store Optimization)

- Experience with backlink or PR-driven SEO

- Data analysis or experimentation background

- Interest in AI search and LLM discoverability



Why This Role Matters



This role directly determines:

- Whether long-term organic growth compounds or stalls

- Whether content becomes an asset or a cost

- Whether BriarBear becomes an authority in its category

- Whether distribution works without relying solely on paid ads



Apply thru this form -

title: Beginner Video Editor (Football Content)
description:
Work From Home | Full-Time | Creative Growth | Long-Term Role

What We Create

We create football content like match highlights, podcast-style discussions, and friends hanging out talking about football. Check this out to see the kind of content you’ll be working on: 





If this is the kind of content you enjoy watching, you’ll love working on it.



Why This Opportunity Is Exciting

$480/month starting pay — get rewarded right from day one

Paid trial tasks — show your skills and earn while you prove yourself

Philippine holidays observed, with extra pay if you choose to work — your effort never goes unnoticed

Identifying key 
nts: reactions, controversial takes, big opinions

Who We’re Looking For

You have a good understanding of football — matches, players, trends, and common fan discussions

Remote, full-time, Monday to Friday, 2:00 PM – 10:00 PM PH Time (flexible as needed).

title: Creative Specialist for a Fast Growing E-Commerce Brand
description:
We are a fast growing greeting card brand selling across Europe. We help people create real connection through beautifully made, responsibly produced cards crafted with care.



We are hiring a Creative Specialist to build a consistent, visually recognizable Amazon presence, so every listing looks like one brand and every product stands out.



This is an ownership role. We run lean, move fast, and collaborate long term. We do not micromanage. We expect you to own outcomes, not just complete tasks.



Why This Role Exists



Our products are already winning. What is missing is a creative system that scales.



Right now, creative execution and testing are not a consistent weekly process. That slows listing improvements, launches, and brand consistency across Amazon.



We are hiring this role to own Amazon creative end to end, so listings and ad assets improve every week, not occasionally.



What You Will Be Responsible For



You will own Amazon creative performance. You will choose priorities each week based on data and business goals. You will not be given step by step instructions.



You will own:

• Brand consistency: clear visual guidelines so every listing looks like one brand and communicates clearly

• Listing and ad creatives: improve listing creatives and produce ad assets that increase CTR and CVR

• Weekly creative testing: run tests, track results, and optimize based on what works



Who This Role Is NOT For



This role is not for:

• Someone looking for a side project or unstable availability

• Someone managing multiple brands or clients at the same time

• Someone who needs daily direction or constant check ins

• Someone who is slow to ship or misses deadlines

• Someone looking for short term freelance work instead of long term partnership



What We Expect From You



We expect:

• Ownership and accountability for outcomes, not tasks

• Clear, proactive communication and early flagging of issues

• Ability to work independently with structure and good judgment

• Strong taste plus attention to detail

• A test, learn, improve mindset with comfort using data

• Long-term collaboration and reliability



How to Apply



Please send:

• Your CV

• Two examples of Amazon listing creatives you improved, with a short explanation of what you changed and why

• Include the word compounding in the first line of your application so we know you read this carefully



Applications that do not follow instructions will not be considered.

title: n8n Automation Specialist
description:
About the role



We are looking for an experienced, hands-on n8n Automation Specialist to build and deploy the core workflows of our company. The goal is to industrialize our operations (content, SEO, marketing, internal ops) with automations that are stable, maintainable, documented, and directly useful to the business.



You can use existing n8n templates and adapt them — we care about real results, reliability, and speed of execution, not reinventing the wheel.



What we are really looking for



We are not looking for someone who is just “trying out” n8n or who only knows how to connect a few nodes.

We are looking for someone who can show workflows that are already built, already working, and already used in a real business context.



Your application will be much stronger if you send examples of workflows that have already helped:

 • save time for a company,

 • automate content production,

 • improve marketing execution,

 • increase operational efficiency,

 • or support business growth through reliable automation.



In other words: we want proof of workflows that are already running and creating real impact, not just theoretical demos.



Responsibilities

 • Design and implement end-to-end workflows in n8n (structure, naming, best practices).

 • Build automations connected to:

 • Webflow (CMS, forms, content updates)

 • OpenAI API (generation/rewriting, extraction, classification, structured outputs)

 • Google APIs (Sheets, Drive, Gmail, Calendar, etc.)

 • SEO blog pipeline (ideas ? brief ? writing ? optimization ? publishing)

 • SEO search / reporting (monitoring + recurring task automation)

 • Social media (content repurposing, scheduling, publishing, reporting)



Current priority needs



We are especially looking for someone who already knows how to build or can quickly deploy workflows for:

 • automated image carousel creation

 • automated Instagram Reel creation

 • content repurposing and transformation for social media

 • content scheduling / publishing / reporting



If you have already built these kinds of workflows, make that very clear in your application and provide concrete examples.



Production standards expected

 • Credentials management and environment variables

 • Error handling, retries, logging, and alerts

 • Testing, documentation, workflow exports, and versioning

 • Clean handover documentation and a proper operational setup



Required skills (must-have)

 • Strong n8n experience (nodes, expressions, webhooks, HTTP Request, data transformation, error handling)

 • Solid API integration experience (OAuth, rate limits, pagination, webhooks)

 • Ability to ship fast and build maintainable systems

 • Ability to deliver workflows that are robust, production-ready, and business-oriented



Nice to have

 • Webflow CMS experience

 • OpenAI best practices (prompting, JSON outputs, guardrails)

 • Google Sheets/Drive automation and reporting

 • Good SEO understanding (on-page, search intent)

 • Social media automation experience

 • Hands-on experience with visual carousel workflows and Instagram Reel workflows



Deliverables

 • Working, exportable, clean, and documented n8n workflows

 • Workflow tracker (goal, status, dependencies, test notes)

 • Standards in place (naming, structure, retries, logs, alerts)

 • Final handover + short walkthrough



Compensation

 • 6 hours/day

 • Fixed monthly rate only

 • No hourly rates



To apply, you MUST include:

 1. Your expected monthly salary for 6 hours/day (USD or PHP)

 2. Whether you can commit for 1 month or 2 months

 3. Your earliest start date + your timezone

 4. 2–3 examples of n8n workflows you have already built, ideally including:

 • screenshots,

 • exports,

 • Loom videos,

 • case studies,

 • and most importantly, a clear explanation of what the workflow did, whether it was actually used in production, and what result it delivered for the business



Important



We will prioritize applications that show real, working workflows already used in a business environment, with a clear impact on productivity, marketing, or company growth.

Generic applications with no concrete proof, no real use cases, or no demonstration of results will not be prioritized.



Potential extension



If the collaboration goes well, this can become a long-term collaboration / recurring partner role for automation in our organization.



Application subject:

“n8n Specialist — Monthly Rate + Portfolio”

title: COLD CALLING & PROSPECTING VA (REAL ESTATE) — HIGH PERFORMANCE ONLY (Part time to Full time)
description:
COLD CALLING & PROSPECTING VA (REAL ESTATE) — HIGH PERFORMANCE ONLY

Hiring a high-level cold caller / prospector with proven experience in high-volume outbound calling.



Must be available: 8am-12pm EST 



This is not an entry-level role.



REQUIREMENTS (DO NOT APPLY IF YOU DON’T MEET THESE):



* 1–2+ years of cold calling / outbound sales experience



* Experience with high-volume dialing (100) 



* Near-native English with a clear, confident speaking voice



* Ability to handle objections, rejection, and gatekeepers



* Experience setting qualified appointments (not just conversations)



* Comfortable working in a fast-paced, performance-driven environment



IMPORTANT — READ THIS CAREFULLY



This is NOT a script-heavy, pressure sales role.



We are not looking for someone who reads a script and pushes a sale.



We are looking for someone who can:



* Have natural, intelligent conversations



* Ask strategic questions to uncover real information



* Listen carefully and respond based on what the prospect is actually saying



* Think in real time — not just follow a script



* Build rapport while still moving the conversation forward



* If your style is rigid or overly scripted, this will not be a fit.



PREFERRED:



Real estate prospecting experience (NYC preferred)



Experience calling US-based clients



Familiarity with dialers (Mojo, Vulcan7, RedX, etc.)



CRM experience (Follow Up Boss or similar)



WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:



High-volume outbound cold calling



Prospecting leads and identifying opportunities



Having real conversations — not reading scripts



Asking smart questions and qualifying prospects



Booking qualified appointments for the team



Updating CRM and tracking activity



TO APPLY (REQUIRED — FOLLOW EXACTLY):



1- Voice Recording (REQUIRED)



Max 30 seconds



Introduce yourself clearly and confidently and tell us why you want this role 



Upload to Google Drive and share the link



2- Experience - Please write your answer in the portal here 

Include:



What you were selling



Daily call volume



Who you were calling (US, real estate, etc.)



Results (appointments set, deals, etc.)

===================================

Salary: 



* $7 /hr 



* Huge performance benefits 



* LONG TERM ROLE / IMMEDIATE HIRE 



==================================

Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.



Only serious, experienced candidates will be considered.

title: Virtual Human Resources (HR) Assistant
description:
I’m looking for a sharp, resourceful, detail-oriented operator who can support the execution of my clients’ job search strategy.



The hours for this job are 8am - 2 pm EST New York. You'll be expected to notify me daily by
 when you start, and again when you leave.



The pay for this job is $5 / hour.



What You’ll Be Doing



Opportunity Research – Identify high-quality roles aligned to each client’s target direction, prioritizing relevance, fit, and strategic alignment over volume.



Resume Alignment – Tailor resumes to reflect the priorities of specific roles, ensuring strong positioning, clarity, and alignment with what employers are actually seeking.



Application Support – Submit applications when needed, ensuring all materials are consistent, polished, and aligned with the target role.



Stakeholder Identification – Identify key individuals connected to each opportunity who have visibility into or influence over the hiring process.



Contact & Profile Research – Locate accurate LinkedIn profiles and professional contact information, maintaining a high standard of precision and reliability.



Outreach Customization – Adapt outreach messages using proven frameworks so they feel personalized, thoughtful, and aligned to the opportunity.



What This Is NOT



This is not high-volume job applying, copy/paste outreach, or generic resume work. This is precision-based execution designed for senior-level professionals.



Who This Is Perfect For



Someone who thinks beyond instructions and uses judgment, writes clearly and professionally, and naturally notices details others miss. You should enjoy making things cleaner, sharper, and more aligned rather than just “getting tasks done.”



How to Apply



Please include a short note on why this role fits you, a brief overview of any relevant experience, and one example of something you’ve written. Include the word “PRECISION” at the top of your application so I know you read this.

title: SEO Specialist / Reputation Management (Reverse SEO) + Website Support
description:
Position Overview:

We are seeking an experienced SEO Specialist with strong knowledge of Reverse SEO (reputation management) to help improve our online visibility and suppress negative search results. This role will also include website editing and development support, including creating and optimizing pages.



Key Responsibilities:



SEO & Reverse SEO:

-Develop and execute SEO strategies to improve rankings for branded and non-branded keywords

-Implement reverse SEO strategies to push down negative or unwanted search results

-Create and manage high-ranking content (blogs, landing pages, backlinks, etc.)

-Conduct keyword research and competitor analysis

-Monitor search performance and provide regular reporting



Website Management & Updates:

-Create new pages and landing pages on our website

-Edit and optimize existing pages for SEO and conversions

-Improve site structure, internal linking, and user experience

-Ensure website content aligns with branding and messaging



Qualifications:

-Proven experience in SEO and Reverse SEO / Online Reputation Management

-Strong understanding of Google ranking factors and algorithms

-Experience with tools such as Ahrefs, SEMrush, Google Search Console, etc.

-Ability to create SEO-optimized content

-Experience with website builders (WordPress, Elementor, or similar preferred)

-Basic understanding of HTML/CSS is a plus

-Strong communication and reporting skills



Bonus Skills (Not Required but Preferred):

-Experience in legal, insurance, or service-based industries

-Paid ads (Google Ads / PPC) knowledge

-Content writing / copywriting

-Local SEO optimization (Google Business Profile, citations, etc.)



What We’re Looking For:

-Someone proactive who can take ownership of our SEO strategy

-Results-driven mindset with a focus on measurable growth

-Ability to identify problems and implement solutions independently



How to Apply:



Please submit:

-Resume or portfolio

-Examples of SEO or reverse SEO work (before/after results preferred)

-Websites you have worked on or managed

-Brief explanation of your approach to reverse SEO

title: Senior Graphic Designer (B2B / LinkedIn / Healthcare & Tech Consulting)
description:
We are looking for a high-level Graphic Designer to support a healthcare and technology consulting firm’s LinkedIn presence.



This is not an entry-level Canva role. We are specifically looking for someone who can create visually elevated, structured, and professional designs that reflect a high-end consulting brand.



Our audience includes healthcare executives, operators, and private equity-backed organizations. The design work must reflect clarity, depth, and credibility not generic social media content.



Responsibilities:

 • Design LinkedIn graphics, carousels, and videos based on written content

 • Translate complex ideas into clear visual frameworks

 • Create layouts that feel structured, layered, and professional

 • Build reusable templates aligned to brand standards

Requirements:

 • Strong experience in graphic design (not virtual assistant work)

 • Clean, modern, editorial-style layouts

title: Figma Web Designer for DTC Ecommerce Brands (Figma, CRO, Shopify)
description:
We're a fast-growing e-commerce agency that helps direct-to-consumer brands in the USA market build high-converting websites that drive serious revenue. We're looking for a talented Figma designer who understands what makes ecommerce pages convert and can create stunning designs that actually sell products.



What you'll do:

• Design complete ecommerce websites from scratch in Figma for multiple DTC brands (supplements, footwear, health products)

• Create high-converting product pages, homepages, landing pages, and advertorials optimized for USA market customers

• Apply proven CRO principles and conversion best practices throughout every design decision

• Develop cohesive branding and visual identity for new ecommerce brands from the ground up

• Design equally well for both desktop and mobile experiences

• Deliver 1-2 complete website designs per month with fast turnaround times



What we're looking for:

• Proven track record designing for DTC ecommerce brands with measurable CRO results you can share

• Expert-level Figma skills including building design systems and components from scratch

• Deep understanding of what makes ecommerce pages convert specifically in the USA market

• Strong visual design abilities combined with a conversion-focused mindset

• Experience developing branding for new ecommerce brands and creating cohesive visual identities

• Portfolio showcasing product pages, landing pages, and advertorials that have driven real sales results



Nice to have:

• Ability to implement your designs in Shopify using theme editors, Replo, or similar page builders

• Copywriting skills for creating compelling ecommerce page content

• Specific experience with supplement, health, or footwear ecommerce brands



This is a part-time position working 20 hours per week with flexible scheduling. You can work during your preferred hours as long as you meet project deadlines. Compensation is $15/hour.



How to Apply:

To make sure you've read this entire post, please start your application with the phrase 'FigmaConvert429'. Then answer the following questions in your application:



1. Walk us through your process for designing a high-converting product page from scratch. How do you d
 on page structure, layout, and which elements to include?

2. What are the top 3 CRO mistakes you see most often on DTC ecommerce websites in the USA market, and how do you solve them in your designs?

3. Have you developed branding and visual identity for ecommerce brands before? If so, share an example and describe your approach to creating a cohesive brand experience.

4. How many hours does it take you to design a mid-length CRO optimized ecommerce product page? 

5. Please share a Figma link containing your best ecommerce website designs, including product pages, homepages, and landing pages you've created.

title: Zapier Automation Specialist — GHL / Stripe / DocuSign / Google Workspace
description:
This is the first of several complex projects. We are looking for someone we can build a long-term working relationship with. 



We are a marketing agency building an automated client onboarding and billing system. We need an experienced Zapier specialist to build and connect a series of workflows across multiple platforms. This is a one-time project with a defined scope — no ongoing commitment needed unless additional work arises.



The stack:

Zapier

GoHighLevel (GHL)

Stripe

DocuSign

Google Drive · Google Workspace



What needs to be built — 3 Zaps + 1 GHL workflow:

Zap 0 — Salesperson submits internal GHL form ? Zapier creates a DocuSign envelope from a pre-built template and sends it to the client automatically. Salesperson never logs into DocuSign.

Zap 1 — Client signs DocuSign agreement ? Zapier creates a Stripe Customer record, creates a $1,000 one-time invoice, passes the unique payment link to GHL, GHL sends it to the client via SMS and
.

Zap 2 — Client pays the Stripe invoice ? Zapier creates a $2,500/month recurring subscription on the saved card, adds authorization metadata to the Stripe customer record, updates the GHL contact, triggers the GHL onboarding sequence.

Zap 0.5 — Client submits GHL onboarding form ? Zapier creates a client folder structure in Google Drive inside a pre-existing root folder, shares the Client Uploads subfolder with
 addresses provided by the client, stores the folder URL back in the GHL contact record.

GHL Workflow — Onboarding form submission triggers internal team notification.



Requirements:

All payment links must be tied to a specific Stripe Customer record — no generic payment links

DocuSign envelope fields must capture name,
, and phone from document fields, not just metadata

Stripe subscription start date must be calculated as 14 days after invoice payment date

Stripe customer metadata must be updated with DocuSign authorization date on subscription creation

Google Drive folder structure is predefined — Zapier creates folders only, no permission management needed for internal folders

Error notifications must be configured for all Zaps

Full documentation of each Zap must be provided on completion



What we will provide:

Full written specification document for each Zap with step-by-step logic

DocuSign template already built

GHL account access

Stripe account access

Google Drive root folder already set up with correct permissions



How we will select the right person:

We will shortlist 3–4 candidates. Each will receive an identical brief for a small test build — paid at $20. We will compare the results and select based on output quality, not interviews. 



Please reply with your responses to the following:



1. Are you using Claude Code and to what level?

2. What are the main issues you typically run into when integrating Zapier with Stripe?

3. What's the most common failure point you see in DocuSign automations?

4. Have you worked with GHL before — how comfortable are you with its Zapier integration?

5. Are you available during Eastern Time 9am–5pm for calls and communication? Do you have a reliable connection with a working Zoom camera and mic?

6. Can you provide a short introductory video before we jump on a Zoom interview?

7. What's your turnaround on a project of this scope and are you available for a short paid test build ($20) before we commit?



We truly look forward to meeting every candidate and a successful long term relationship with the right one.

title: AI Automation Specialist: $6 to $8 per hour
description:
AI Automation Specialist | Position Type: Part-time | Experience Level: At least 2 Years 



About the Role

Join our expanding organization as an AI & Automation Specialist and become one of the key drivers of our digital transformation journey. In this unique hybrid role, you'll joint our team to architect innovative automation solutions while simultaneously building internal expertise across our team. You're not just implementing technology — you're contributing to empower our entire organization to work smarter.



This is an ideal opportunity for someone who thrives at the intersection of technology and people development, combining hands-on technical implementation with strategic education and organizational change management.



What You'll Do:

Technical Implementation & Optimization

+ Design, implement, and maintain sophisticated automation workflows using industry-leading platforms (Make, Zapier, n8n)

+ Integrate AI solutions strategically across departments to drive measurable productivity gains

+ Build complex, multi-step workflows featuring API integrations and webhooks

+ Continuously troubleshoot, optimize, and enhance existing automation processes

+ Stay ahead of emerging AI technologies and evaluate their applicability to organizational needs



Knowledge Transfer & Education:

+ Develop and deliver customized training programs that enable tea
mbers to confidently use AI tools and automation platforms in their daily roles

+ Create comprehensive, step-by-step documentation and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) that empower independence

+ Produce engaging training materials, video tutorials, and resource guides

+ Provide ongoing mentorship and support as the organization scales



Cross-Functional Collaboration:

+ Partner with department leaders to identify automation opportunities and pain points

+ Translate business requirements into technical solutions

+ Champion a culture of innovation and continuous improvement throughout the organization





Required Technical Qualifications

Automation & Integration Expertise:

+ Hands-on proficiency with Make (Integromat), Zapier, and/or n8n (minimum 2+ years of demonstrated experience)

+ Solid understanding of API integrations, webhooks, and data flows

+ Proven ability to design and execute complex, multi-step automation workflows

+ Experience troubleshooting and optimizing existing processes



AI & Emerging Technologies:

+ Working knowledge of generative AI platforms including ChatGPT, Claude AI, Perplexity, and similar tools

+ Understanding of AI capabilities, limitations, and appropriate use cases

+ Familiarity with emerging AI applications relevant to business operations

+ Ability to evaluate and recommend new tools as technology evolves



Project & Information Management:

+ Proficiency with project management and workflow organization tools (Trello or similar platforms)

+ Strong documentation and SOP creation skills

+ Ability to organize and prioritize multiple concurrent projects





Required Soft Skills & Personal Qualities

+ Excellent Communicator: Translates complex technical concepts into clear, accessible language for non-technical audiences

+ Strategic Educator: Passionate about teaching others and genuinely invested in their success

+ Proactive & Ambitious: Takes initiative, identifies improvement opportunities, and continuously raises the bar

+ Adaptable & Flexible: Thrives in fast-paced environments and embraces evolving priorities with a growth mindset

+ Reliable & Results-Oriented: Committed to delivering high-quality work and following through on commitments

+ Collaborative Team Player: Works effectively in small, growing teams while also functioning independently

+ Strong Multitasker: Comfortable managing competing priorities without losing attention to detail





What Success Looks Like



In your first 90 days, you'll:

+ Audit existing processes and identify top automation opportunities

+ Implement 2-3 high-impact automation workflows

+ Deliver initial training to key departments

+ Establish documentation standards and create foundational SOPs



Within 6 months, you should:

+ Have significantly expanded automation across the organization

+ Built a culture where tea
mbers confidently use AI and automation tools

+ Established yourself as the go-to expert for digital transformation initiatives

+ Developed training materials that enable semi-independent learning





Why Join Us?

+ Strategic Impact: Shape how an entire organization adopts and scales AI and automation technologies

+ Growth Trajectory: Join an expanding company where your contributions directly influence company success

+ Innovation Culture: Work with cutting-edge technologies and stay at the forefront of AI implementation

+ Meaningful Work: Make a tangible difference across the entire organization—from individual productivity to strategic operations

+ Development Opportunities: In a growing company, there's significant room for career progression and skill expansion

+ Collaborative Environment: Work with a team that values innovation, continuous learning, and mutual support





What We're Looking For

The ideal candidate is someone who genuinely loves solving problems with automation and AI, stays curious about emerging technologies, and gets energized by helping others succeed. You should be comfortable wearing multiple hats, working independently when needed, and thriving in an environment that rewards initiative and continuous improvement. If you're both a "builder" and a "teacher," this role is for you.



How to Apply

Please submit the following materials:

+ Your Resume – Highlighting relevant automation and AI experience

+ Portfolio or Examples – Specific automation workflows you've built or contributed to (feel free to share links, screenshots, or descriptions). Highlight complexity and impact.

+ Experience Summary – A brief description of:

- Your hands-on experience with Make, Zapier, n8n, or similar platforms

- Examples of AI tools you've implemented or trained others on

- Your approach to teaching technical concepts to non-technical tea
mbers

- What drew you to this type of hybrid technical/educational role



Your Story – In a few sentences, tell us why you're genuinely excited about this opportunity and what you hope to accomplish



About Us

We're a growing organization committed to leveraging AI and automation to work smarter, not just harder. We believe in continuous learning, collaborative problem-solving, and building a culture where technology empowers every tea
mber.

title: Customer Service Representative
description:
US-Based Goat Milk Skincare Brand



We’re a growing U.S.-based company that creates goat milk soaps, lotions, and skincare products. Our customers value quality, transparency, and thoughtful support — and we’re looking for someone who does too.

This role goes beyond answering messages. You’ll be responsible for understanding the real problem a customer is experiencing and delivering the right solution, even when the customer doesn’t explain it clearly.



Responsibilities

Support customers via phone,
, chat, and limited social media

Assist with orders, products, shipping, and website-related issues

Identify root causes and distinguish user error vs. system issues

Calm and de-escalate frustrated customers professionally

Recommend correct products or next steps — even when the customer is mistaken

Share helpful information customers may not know to ask for

Work across multiple systems to fully resolve issues

Provide feedback to improve customer experience and processes



Tools You’ll Use

Shopify, Klaviyo, Google Sheets, 
, Canva

(LiveHelpNow may be added; phone system will change soon)



Schedule

Shifts will fall within a defined coverage window and may vary

Customer support is provided 7 days a week

Coverage hours are 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM Eastern Time



What We’re Looking For

Strong problem-solving and judgment

Excellent written English (grammar and spelling matter)

Clear, neutral accent for phone-based support

Calm, positive demeanor — even with difficult customers

Ability to adapt responses based on customer attitude and value

Comfortable telling customers they’re wrong kindly and clearly

Willing to ask questions and seek feedback

Able to navigate multiple tools to get the full picture



If you enjoy thinking critically, helping people properly, and being trusted to make good decisions — we’d love to hear from you.



To apply, please fill out the form below.

title: Senior Electrical Engineer – MEP & Energy Calculations
description:
We are seeking a Senior Electrical Engineer to lead electrical design and calculations for complex architectural and MEP projects. This is a high-impact role focused on ensuring compliance with Florida Building Codes, energy performance standards, and accurate engineering documentation across multiple disciplines. This is a remote position, supporting US-based projects in the EST timezone.



Key Responsibilities:



Perform electrical engineering calculations, including load analysis, circuit design, lighting, and energy performance compliance.



Review and validate MEP calculations from internal teams and external consultants.



Collaborate with architectural, mechanical, and plumbing teams to coordinate multi-discipline designs.



Ensure Life Safety, Means of Egress, and ADA compliance for electrical systems.



Contribute to permit-ready Construction Document (CD) sets and ensure adherence to Miami / Florida Building Codes.



Review technical specifications, quantity takeoffs, and CSI documentation for accuracy.



Estimate project hours and prepare detailed scopes of work.



Lead and mentor remote production teams (minimum 2 years of people management experience required).



Support energy modeling, performance compliance, and sustainability documentation.



Required Experience:



5+ years in Electrical Engineering within AEC, construction, or MEP environments.



Proven experience producing and reviewing permit-ready CD sets.



Strong knowledge of Miami / Florida Building Codes.



Experience coordinating across architectural and MEP disciplines.



2+ years managing engineering or production teams.



US-based project experience preferred.



Proficiency in BIM, Revit, AutoCAD, and construction documentation workflows.



Preferred Qualifications:



Licensed Professional Engineer (PE) in Electrical Engineering.



Experience supporting US permitting workflows.



Experience with remote engineering production environments.



Work Setup:



Fully remote



EST timezone availability required



We are looking for a structured, detail-oriented technical leader who can deliver accurate calculations, permit-ready designs, and cross-disciplinary coordination under Florida code requirements. If you are an experienced electrical engineer with leadership experience and a track record of high-quality engineering deliverables, we want to connect with you.

title: Lead Transactions Coordinator & Executive Assistant
description:
I’m a real estate entrepreneur based in Dallas, Texas, running two connected businesses: a real estate sales team that helps buyers and sellers, and a property management company that operates furnished co-living rentals. We use ClickUp for task and project management and Google Drive as our primary file system. We also use Buildium as our main property management tool.



I’m looking for a full-time Real Estate Lead Transaction Coordinator to manage the flow of transactions from contract to close, making sure deadlines are met and nothing falls through the cracks.



This role combines transaction management with systems coordination. You’ll handle communication between agents, clients, title, and lenders — while also ensuring that files and documents for each transaction are organized and fully documented.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

- Manage all real estate transactions from contract to close

- Communicate with agents, clients, title companies, and lenders to ensure smooth closings

- Schedule showings, enter listings, and draft proposals

- Track deadlines, contingencies, and documentation compliance

- Create and update SOPs and workflow documentation for transaction processes

- Coordinate with automation partners for system improvements



YOU’D BE A GREAT FIT IF

- 2+ years of experience as a Real Estate Transaction Coordinator (or a similar role in real estate operations)

- You follow instructions exactly and don’t cut corners

- You write and speak clearly in English

- You stay calm managing many moving parts

- You take ownership and don’t need hand-holding

- You take initiative: you spot issues early, investigate, and propose next steps/solutions

- You use good judgment and can prioritize without being told every step

- You thrive in a fast-paced environment and can adapt quickly as priorities shift

- You are tech-savvy, detail-oriented, and reliable under deadlines

- Understanding of contract timelines, title/lender coordination, and client communication

- Flexibility to work some hours on weekends is preferred





ROLE DETAILS

- Full-time, remote

- Schedule: Mon–Fri, aligned with CST

- Pay: $5-7 USD/hour, based on experience

- Long-term role with growth and bonuses



HOW TO APPLY (READ CAREFULLY)

We do not review platfor
ssages.

Applications that don’t follow instructions are disqualified.



STEP 1 —

1. Send an
 to 

2. Subject line: Lead Transactions Coordinator – [Full Name]

3.
 body must include only:

- A short greeting

- A Google Drive folder link

- Your answer to the Detail Check (see Step 3)



STEP 2 — GOOGLE DRIVE FOLDER

1. Create a folder named: [Your Full Name] — Lead Transactions Coordinator Application

2. Set sharing to “Anyone with the link can view.”

3. You can organize the folder however you think is best. I will be looking at both what you submit and how you organize it.

4. Include the following:

a. Resume (PDF)

b. Tech and Setup

   - Include a screenshot of your internet speed showing download and upload speeds. 

   - Include a screenshot of your computer specs showing RAM, processor, and OS.

   - Include one photo of your current workspace (desk and computer).

c. Written Answers (one Google Doc)

   - Transactions Coordinator Experience

      - Describe one property management role or company you worked with. Include:

      - Number of properties/units

      - Your day-to-day responsibilities

      - Tools you used 

   - Taking Ownership

      - We already have SOPs and systems, but they’re still improving.

      - What would you focus on in your first 2 weeks to keep operations running smoothly without needing constant supervision?

d. Systems Proof (2–4 screenshots)

   - At least 1 “HQ” or home base view you’ve built (for a founder/team)

      Example: a dashboard, control panel, or main view in ClickUp, Asana, Notion, etc.

   - At least 1 project management board or folder structure you created

      Example: a tool for maintenance tracking, tenant or leasing pipeline, or vendor coordination

   - For each screenshot, add a short caption (this can be in a small Google Doc or directly on the image as text), including:

      - What the system is used for

      - Which tool it’s in (ClickUp, Asana, Google Drive, Notion, etc.)

      - What part you personally designed or organized

   - If you cannot share screenshots from a previous client because of confidentiality, you may recreate a similar structure in a fresh workspace (with fake data) and take screenshots of that instead.

e. Video Introduction (2–4 minutes)

   - Who you are and where you’re based

   - Your Transactions Coordinator experience (+team leading)

   - Example of handling multiple stakeholders at once

   - How you personally stay organized



STEP 3 — DETAIL CHECK

At the very bottom of your
 (under the Drive link), write only the price per square foot for this property:



3384 P Avenue, Plano, TX 75074 – Sold for 255,555 USD.



Just include the final price per square foot number, with no explanation.



WHAT HAPPENS NEXT

-
 application screen

- Systems portfolio review

- Paid sample task (1-2 hours)

- Interview

- Hire

title: Shopify Store Manager - Fashion
description:
Dear,



We are looking for an experienced and driven Shopify Store Manager to lead our frontend operations full-time. In 2025, we generated $12M+ in revenue and are continuing to scale as a fast-growing fashion dropshipping company. We are looking for a hands-on leader who can manage a team, optimize performance, and take full ownership of our frontend operations.



About the Role: 

You will be responsible for leading a team of 10 people, including product listers, media buyers, video editors, ads launchers, and photo editors. You will oversee the full frontend operation from store performance and conversion rate optimization to ad creatives and new store builds.



MUST HAVE EXPERIENCE WITH:



- Shopify (store management, store builds, CRO)

- Klaviyo 
 marketing and flows)

- Fashion dropshipping (managing stores in this niche specifically)

- Frontend operations (ads, creatives, funnels, product pages)

- Leading and coordinating a marketing/creative team



Responsibilities:



1) Managing and leading a team of 10 across multiple frontend departments.

2) Overseeing product listing, ad creative production, and media buying.

3) Optimizing conversion rates across all active stores.

4) Building and launching new Shopify stores from the ground up.

5) Tracking KPIs across departments and improving processes continuously.

6) Coordinating between media buyers, editors, and designers to ensure aligned output.

7) Identifying bottlenecks and implementing systems to improve team efficiency.



Applicants must:



- Have proven experience managing Shopify stores in a fashion or dropshipping context.

- Have experience leading a team of at least 5+ people.

- Be available full-time and during weekends

- Be organized, process-driven, and results-oriented.

- Be available to start immediately or within short notice.



Application Instructions:



If you believe you are a good fit, please apply with a short introduction video of yourself (be original, do not read from a script).



Answer the following questions in your application (not in your video):



1) How many years of experience do you have with Shopify?

2) What was your most recent role, and what were your day-to-day responsibilities?

3) What type of e-commerce or dropshipping stores did you manage (markets, niches, number of stores)?

4) Do you have experience with Klaviyo? If yes, describe how you have used it. 

5) Have you managed or built Shopify stores in the fashion niche specifically?

6) Can you describe your experience with frontend operations (ads, creatives, funnels, product pages)?

7) What are your experiences with Meta and Google ads?

8) Are you available full-time?

9) Are you available on weekends if needed?

10) Do you currently have other jobs or clients?

11) When can you start?



We are looking for someone who takes full ownership, leads with clarity, and consistently drives results.



I look forward to your proposal!



Best regards, 

Tess

title: Head of Finance & Accounting
description:
Location: Remote (Philippines) – Must work U.S. hours



About Us



WA DeHart Inc., a U.S.-based wholesale distribution company headquartered in Pennsylvania, has been in business for over 100 years. With new leadership, capital, and technology, we are transforming into a tech-driven, growth-focused distribution platform serving convenience stores, gas stations, and smoke shops across the U.S.



Our industry is low-margin, high-volume, and heavily regulated, requiring precision, compliance, and operational discipline to succeed.



Role Overview



We are seeking an experienced Head of Finance & Accounting to oversee all accounting operations, compliance, and financial strategy for our growing organization.



This is a hands-on executive leadership role for a detail-oriented professional who can build scalable financial systems, maintain strict regulatory compliance, and partner with leadership on growth initiatives including cost optimization, capital raises, and acquisitions.



Key Responsibilities



Oversee all accounting operations across multiple entities, ensuring accuracy, transparency, and compliance



Develop and maintain strong financial controls, policies, and scalable accounting systems



Manage monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes, including preparation of financial statements and reports



Lead accounting, financial reporting, and compliance across U.S. operations, ensuring adherence to U.S. and Pennsylvania regulations; experience with FDA regulations, tobacco compliance, and PAC compliance is a strong asset



File required monthly reports with the Pennsylvania Department of Revenue and manage regulatory filings and audits



Partner with external auditors, CPAs, and accounting firms to produce reviewed or audited financial statements



Manage bank and lender relationships, including reporting requirements and loan covenant compliance



Support executive leadership with capital raises, investor reporting, and KPI monitoring



Lead budgeting, forecasting, cash flow management, and financial planning



Drive cost-cutting initiatives and operational efficiency, including vendor contract review, renegotiation of terms, and identifying savings opportunities



Represent the company in audits, tax reviews, and accounting disputes



Mentor and develop a small team of accountants/bookkeepers



Provide financial insights that support organic growth and potential acquisitions



Continuously improve the efficiency, scalability, and accuracy of financial operations



Perform other finance and accounting responsibilities as needed to support strategic, operational, and compliance objectives



Qualifications



10+ years of progressive experience in finance and accounting leadership roles



CPA, CA, or equivalent professional designation strongly preferred



Strong knowledge of financial reporting, regulatory compliance, and accounting controls



Experience managing vendor relationships, cost optimization initiatives, and financial efficiency projects



Experience working with banks, lenders, and financial institutions, including loan covenant compliance



Experience supporting capital raises, investor reporting, or financing activities



Strong business acumen with the ability to think like an owner



Highly detail-oriented, process-driven, and capable of building scalable financial systems



Experience leading and mentoring accounting teams



Preferred Experience



Experience managing multi-entity accounting environments



Experience in wholesale distribution, FMCG, or similar industries



What We Offer



Competitive compensation



Executive leadership role with direct impact on company growth



Opportunity to work closely with ownership and executive leadership



Career growth as the company expands into multiple locations



Long-term opportunities for growth within a scaling U.S. organization



Application Requirement



Please include a short voice or video recording briefly introducing yourself when submitting your application. Applications without a recording will not be considered.

title: Chief Financial Officer (CFO)
description:

Location: Remote (Philippines) ???????? — Must work U.S. hours



???? Company Overview

We are a growing real estate development and investment company focused on warehouse distribution facilities, commercial leasing, and ground-up development projects.

Our projects involve land acquisition, construction and development, tenant leasing, and long-term asset management.

We are seeking an experienced Chief Financial Officer (CFO) to lead financial strategy, capital raising, project financing, and investor fund creation to support our expansion.



???? Position Summary

The CFO will oversee all financial operations including project financing, investor fund structuring, bank lending relationships, construction finance, and real estate financial management.

This role requires strong experience in warehouse or commercial real estate development and capital markets.

The CFO will work closely with the CEO to secure financing, structure investment vehicles, and drive financial growth across multiple development projects.



???? Key Responsibilities



Strategic Financial Leadership

• Partner with the CEO to develop financial strategy for real estate development and expansion

• Oversee financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and capital allocation

• Evaluate project feasibility and financial returns



???? Capital Raising & Investor Funds

• Structure and launch private investment funds for real estate projects

• Raise capital from institutional investors, family offices, and high-net-worth investors

• Design equity and debt investment structures for development projects

• Manage investor relations and reporting



???? Debt Financing & Bank Relationships

• Identify and secure financing from commercial banks, private lenders, and financial institutions

• Negotiate and close construction loans, bridge financing, and long-term financing

• Manage lender relationships and loan compliance



???? Ground-Up Development Financial Management

• Oversee financial aspects of new site development projects

• Create financial models for land acquisition, development costs, and projected returns

• Monitor project budgets, costs, and profitability



???? Leasing & Asset Financial Performance

• Analyze lease structures, rental income projections, and tenant profitability

• Work with leasing teams to optimize cash flow and asset value



???? Accounting & Tax Compliance

description:
Medical Operations & Reporting Virtual Assistant

Remote | 30 hrs/week | $6/hour



Overview:



We are seeking a Virtual Medical Assistant (VMA) to oversee the overall operational health of our clinic. This is a high-level role for a professional who functions as a direct extension of the client. You won't just record data; you will analyze it to provide the insights the client needs to run the practice effectively.



The ideal candidate possesses a strong background in medical coding, financial oversight, and team coordination. You will be responsible for ensuring that clinical documentation, billing accuracy, and patient trends are translated into actionable reports that drive the clinic’s success.





Key Responsibilities

? Review Explanation of Benefits (EOBs): Ensure accuracy in billing and insurance

documentation

? SOAP Notes: Monitor compliance and accuracy, ensuring timely and complete

documentation.

? Appointments: Track missed and canceled appointments

? Reports: Generate and distribute timely reports on outstanding balances and other

operational metrics with insights and recommendations

? Team Coordination: Facilitate seamless communication across staff and providers



Required Soft Skills

? Proactivity: Anticipate scheduling conflicts, outstanding balance, and patient

needs before they escalate.

? Attention to Detail: Ensure completion and accuracy in SOAP notes, EOB reviews,

and financial reports.

? Executive Presence: Communicate with confidence and professionalism when

coordinating with providers and tea
mbers.

? Anticipating Needs: Identify potential gaps in clinic operations and propose

solutions to improve efficiency.

? Critical Thinking: Analyze appointment trends, billing issues, and workflow

challenges to recommend actionable improvements.



Mandatory Requirements

? Minimum of 1 year experience as a Medical Virtual Assistant.

? Exposure to CPT and ICD?10 codes.

? Proven leadership experience

? Must own and use a Mac OS device (MacBook/iMac). Windows devices are not

accepted for this role.



Nice to Have

? Prior experience supporting a chiropractic clinic.

title: WordPress SEO and AEO Specialist
description:
About Us:

Day1 Agency is a New York based digital marketing and creative agency. We are building a full website for a wellness and recovery center in Canada and need a highly skilled SEO and AEO specialist to own the full search optimization from the ground up. This is a serious project for a client in a competitive high stakes industry. We need someone genuinely excellent, not just competent.



About the Project:

New WordPress website for a Canadian wellness and addiction recovery center. The site is being designed and built from scratch. Your role is to ensure it ranks, gets found, and shows up in AI search results from day one.



What You Will Own:

Full technical SEO audit and implementation on a new WordPress build

Keyword research targeting high intent searches in the wellness and healthcare space in Canada

On page SEO optimization across all pages including meta titles, descriptions, headings, and content structure

Schema markup and structured data implementation

AEO optimization so the site appears in AI search tools like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI overviews

Google Search Console and Google Analytics 4 setup and configuration

Yoast or Rank Math setup and full configuration

Page speed and core web vitals optimization

Directory and citation building relevant to Canadian healthcare

Monthly monitoring, ranking tracking, and performance reporting



What We Are Looking For:

Minimum 3 years of WordPress SEO experience with real results to show

Demonstrated knowledge of AEO and AI search optimization, this is not optional, we will ask you about this specifically

Experience with healthcare, medical, or professional services websites is a strong plus

Deep understanding of technical SEO not just on page basics

Someone who thinks strategically about search, not just someone who fills in meta descriptions

Strong communicator who can explain their work clearly and proactively

Available for ongoing monthly work after the initial build



This Is Not the Right Role If:

You only know basic on page SEO

You have never heard of AEO or answer engine optimization

You cannot show real examples of rankings or traffic you have moved

You disappear after sending your application



To Apply Please Include:

A brief explanation of how you approach AEO and optimizing for AI search tools like ChatGPT and Perplexity. This is mandatory. Applications without this will not be considered.

Two to three examples of SEO work you have done with measurable results, traffic growth, ranking improvements, or lead generation

Your monthly rate for ongoing work

Your availability and typical response time



Compensation:

Competitive rate based on experience. We pay on time, every time. We treat our team with respect and value long term relationships. If you are excellent we will keep working together across many projects.

title: Long Form Video Editor
description:
About the Role

We are looking for a YouTube video editor to join our media team and help produce high-quality long-form content for a fast-growing entrepreneurship and financial education platform.

Our content is designed for entrepreneurs and business owners looking to grow their companies, access capital, and scale their operations.

This role will primarily focus on editing:

• Podcast episodes

• Educational talking-head videos

The initial workload is light and manageable, typically consisting of 1–2 long-form videos per week, with the opportunity for the role to expand as our content production continues to grow.

We are looking for someone who wants to build a long-term working relationship with a creator and brand, not just complete one-off projects.

• Must be available to work EST hours, and all interviews will be conducted during EST business hours.

________________________________________

What You'll Be Working On

Each week you may be responsible for editing content such as:

Podcast Episodes

• Clean up dead space and tighten pacing

• Add intro hook clips at the beginning of the episode

• Insert commercials or call-to-action segments

• Add simple graphics such as social media handles or text callouts when needed

• Deliver a clean, YouTube-ready final episode

Talking-Head Educational Videos

• Edit educational videos typically between 8–15 minutes long

• Remove pauses and tighten pacing

• Add graphics, text, or visual enhancements when appropriate

• Maintain a clean and engaging viewing experience

Our goal is to continuously improve the quality and performance of our long-form content, so we value editors who think about viewer engagement and storytelling, not just cutting clips together.

________________________________________

Example Editing Styles We Like

We draw inspiration from creators and platforms such as:

• Alex Hormozi

• Dan Henry

• Karlton Dennis

• Earn Your Leisure

• The Calum Johnson Show

You don't need to replicate these styles exactly, but familiarity with modern YouTube editing patterns is important.

________________________________________

Thumbnail Creation (Bonus Skill)

If you have experience creating YouTube thumbnails, that is a strong bonus.

We currently use simple thumbnail styles and templates, so this would not require complex design work.

________________________________________

Workload

The typical workload will involve editing approximately:

1–2 long-form videos per week

Occasionally there may be weeks where additional videos are edited to help batch content ahead of schedule.

The workload is designed to remain manageable while maintaining high editing quality.

________________________________________

Compensation

Compensation for this role ranges between:

$600 – $900 per month depending on experience.

As our content production grows, this role has the potential to expand in both responsibility and compensation.

Payments are issued via Wise.

________________________________________

Ideal Candidate

We are looking for someone who:

• Has experience editing YouTube videos or podcasts

• Understands pacing, storytelling, and viewer engagement

• Can maintain clean, professional edits

• Communicates clearly and reliably

• Is interested in working with a creator and brand long-term

Bonus if you also have experience with:

• Motion graphics

• Thumbnail design

• Retention-focused editing styles

________________________________________

Tools We Prefer

Most editors in this role typically use tools such as:

• Adobe Premiere Pro

• After Effects

• DaVinci Resolve

• Photoshop (for thumbnails)

________________________________________

Application Instructions

When applying, please include:

1.	Links to 3 YouTube videos or podcast edits you have worked on

2.	A brief explanation of your role in editing those videos

3.	Your favorite YouTube creator from an editing perspective and why

Applications without examples of long-form editing work will not be considered.

________________________________________



Please complete the full application form below. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.

title: Digital Creator for Non-Profit Organization
description:
Position: Digital Creator



About Us

We are a dedicated non-profit organization seeking a skilled and creative Digital Creator to join our team. Our mission is to create impactful and meaningful content that aligns with our Biblical Christian values.



Job Description

In this role, you will be responsible for creating and designing digital pages that include elements such as ephemera, background papers, pockets, and folds. You will also design the flow of the project to match the biblical lesson using given scripture references. It is crucial that clip art pages accompany the packets and blend nicely with the junk journal, matching the verse and including the verse for crafters to use in their creations. Additionally, all color swatches must coordinate cohesively and align with the theme of each month's packet and lesson.



As a Digital Creator, you will be responsible for producing high-quality digital content using Adobe, MidJourney, Canva, and AI image tools. You will work independently, manage your assignments through Trello, and maintain clear communication with our team via FB Messenger and Trello. This is a project-based role, and compensation will be provided per completed project. We create digital junk journal projects to match Biblical lessons. Knowledge of folio kits, graphics, templates would be of great advantage.



Responsibilities

• Create digital pages using Adobe, MidJourney, Canva Layering, and AI image tools.

• Ensure all content is high-quality and aligns with our organization's values.

• Communicate effectively with the team through FB Messenger and Trello.

• Upload completed projects to Google Drive in various formats (PDF, JPEG with and without watermark, and mockup of

product).

• Complete a test project to demonstrate skills and capabilities.



Requirements

• Proficiency in Adobe, Advanced Canva Skills, and AI image tools.

• Strong English language skills.

• Ability to work independently and manage assignments through Trello.

• Background in Biblical Christian values.

• Good communication skills and timely project completion.



Compensation

You will be compensated at a rate of $1.00 per completed page. Each project comprises 30 to 50 pages. Payments will be made through PayPal upon project completion, approval, and necessary revisions, in compliance with U.S. and our non-profit status. Qualified candidate will also be eligible to receive quarterly bonus based on team outcomes.



Application Process

To apply, please submit your resume and portfolio to 
 and once we review this, we will determine if you will be sent the project. We are looking for candidates who demonstrate excellent communication skills in English, the ability to meet deadlines, and a strong alignment with our mission and Christian values.



We look forward to reviewing your application and potentially welcoming you to our team.



Sincerely,

Tammy Becker

Founder of You Ministries
salary: per project payment and Quartey bonus...

title: Review and Reputation Management Specialist
description:
Review & Reputation Management Specialist

Full-Time | Remote | Gear Elevation



Gear Elevation is growing fast—and as we expand our product lines and marketplace presence, we are looking for a dedicated Review & Reputation Management Specialist who will fully own and elevate our brand reputation across all major review platforms.

This role ensures our customers see Gear Elevation as a trusted brand by increasing our ratings, managing incoming reviews, and building strong relationships with vendors and internal teams.



ROLE OVERVIEW

The Review & Reputation Management Specialist will be responsible for managing, improving, and maintaining Gear Elevation’s online reputation. This includes Trustpilot, Sitejabber, BBB, marketplace reviews, and internal review systems. The specialist will also handle review uploads for new and existing listings and coordinate closely with vendors and customer service teams.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

1. Reputation & Review Platform Management

 • Manage and optimize Gear Elevation’s profiles on Trustpilot, Sitejabber, BBB, and other review platforms.

 • Implement strategies to increase star ratings, improve credibility, and boost customer trust.

 • Monitor daily incoming reviews (100+ per day), classify and moderate them based on internal standards.

 • Handle the workflow for negative reviews (1–3 stars), including escalation and coordination with customer support.

 • Ensure timely responses to reviewers using approved templates and consistent brand voice.

2. Review Publishing for Product Listings

 • Add and update 4–5 star reviews for new and existing listings across all Gear Elevation sales channels.

 • Maintain accuracy of reviews when product updates, bundles, or new variations are launched.

 • Develop and maintain a centralized Review Library for marketing, ads, creati ves, and listing teams.

3. Review Request Flow & Automation

 • Manage and optimize review request automations for post-purchase
s and SMS.

 • Collaborate with Customer Support to improve internal review-handling processes.

 • Monitor Loox, TrustPilot, and internal tools for performance and adjust workflows accordingly.

4. Vendor & Internal Team Coordination

 • Work closely with vendors and fulfillment teams to address product issues that affect reviews.

 • Forward relevant review insights or concerns to product, listing, QA, or operations teams.

 • Gather required documents or details for review disputes, verifications, or product updates.

5. Reporting & Insights

 • Prepare weekly reports covering:

 • Review volume and sentiment

 • Trustpilot/Sitejabber rating trends

 • Top issues, customer feedback patterns

 • Opportunities for improvement

 • Track KPIs for sentiment improvement, review turnaround time, and listing review health.



QUALIFICATIONS

 • Experience in eCommerce, reputation management, moderation, or customer experience preferred.

 • Familiarity with platforms such as Trustpilot, Sitejabber, BBB, Loox, 
, Shopify, TikTok Shop.

 • Excellent written English and attention to detail.

 • Highly organized and can manage a high volume of daily reviews.

 • Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving.

 • Ability to coordinate across multiple departments and external vendors.

 • Proactive and able to work independently.



SUCCESS METRICS / KPIs

 • Trustpilot, Sitejabber, and BBB star rating improvements
